summary: The application automatically creates a privileged admin account with the predictable credentials admin/admin123 during bootstrap. Any external user who knows or guesses these default credentials can authenticate as the administrator and access privileged user-management and course-management functionality.

evidence_snippets.reason: The application invokes the admin seeding routine automatically during normal startup.
The bootstrap logic inserts a fixed administrator username and a predictable password if the account does not already exist.
The standard login path accepts any valid credentials, so the auto-seeded default admin credentials are directly exploitable.

root_cause: The application ships with a built-in privileged account and predictable credentials, then provisions that account automatically during bootstrap instead of requiring secure first-run initialization.
repair_plan: Remove the hard-coded administrator credentials from source code. Replace bootstrap seeding with an explicit one-time initialization flow that requires a deployment-provided secret or environment-controlled initial password, and do not create a privileged account automatically on every application startup.
